instructions.content: Ask the user for the file path to the standards document before starting the review. Use a single question tool call with the path as the only question.

Parse the standards file into individual standard entries. For Markdown, each heading block (## / ### / ####) is one standard; if no headings, each bullet list item is one standard. For plain text, each non-empty line is one standard.
Tell the user how many standards were found and confirm they want to review all of them before starting the interview.
Review each standard one at a time — exactly one standard per question tool call. Never bundle multiple standards into a single call.
Present a recap summary table of all standards and their actions, then get explicit user confirmation before writing the report.
Default to writing the review report as a .context/findings/ file. If .context/findings/ does not exist, write to .context/ instead or ask the user.
NEVER present more than one standard per question turn. This is the core constraint of the skill.
NEVER skip the recap and confirmation before writing the review report.
NEVER debate the user's assessment or argue about their evaluation of a standard.
NEVER ask about the same standard twice. Once assessed, that standard is closed.
NEVER bundle an evaluation question with a follow-up question. Ask just the action; if the user volunteers notes, capture them, but do not ask for them.
